title: PvP Checklist (Things to Do Before Fighting)
summary: Pre-battle checklist for Battlefield (BoG/BoC), Server War (SvS) and Kill Event (KE): what defenders, presets, buffs, gear/talent/treasure/blazon and culture to switch before fighting, plus SvS/KE-only ghost prep.
content: BASICS before any PvP (BoG/BoC/SvS/KE): switch a development defender (e.g. Shajar) to a COMBAT defender; turn OFF subordinate-city linkages ('automatic fight') if you intend to ghost all troops; review march presets (e.g. when using a march-size-increase item); turn off rally notifications (Settings>Options>Alliance War Setting) so ghosting doesn't flood chat; use 1-hour buff items (Attack/Defense/HP/March-size) and set a timer since they expire in 1h; switch Monarch Gear presets per troop type; switch Monarch Talent combat-vs-development presets (esp. the Lv22 Subordinate City Attack talent); switch Civilization Treasure (Globus Cruciger=healing speed, or Tutankhamun's Dagger=march speed to monsters for BoG); switch Blazon (mounted PvP set-buff); activate Shrine 'King's Protection' offer buffs; swap castle decorations that carry buffs; use Alliance Science skills (e.g. 'Mighty Force' = +attack); use the Dragon active skill (high-level, 3rd talent); change Culture to Japan (offense) or Russia (defense) for the subcity buff; switch Mayors if leveling via mayor. SvS/KE ONLY: spend resources first (essential before ghosting the whole army, and safe even otherwise in case you lose the defense); REMOVE the defender if you ghost the whole army, otherwise the defense general dies during the defense.

title: Battlefield: Clash of Civilizations (CoC)
summary: SvS-style 2-hour battlefield: 8 servers = 8 teams; each server's top-25 CoC-Assault players qualify. Score by occupying buildings + killing enemy troops/monsters. Killed main-city troops are wounded (not dead) and auto-heal after.
content: 8 servers form 8 teams and fight for 2 hours; only each server's top-25 from the 'Clash of Civilizations - Assault' event may enter. On entry you land in a Safe Zone with a brief prep period (no attack/teleport) and everyone receives a 15-minute Truce Agreement (some war actions cancel it). During the battle all KILLED main-city troops become WOUNDED (not dead), resources are NOT plundered, healing is cheap/fast, and all wounded auto-heal after the battle (traps and subcity troops excluded). Leaving is permanent: you cannot re-enter, score resets to 0 and no rewards. Periodic Auxiliary Buff grants free troops scaled to your current in-battle server ranking (first & last place get more); those troops vanish on leaving. SCORING = occupy buildings + kill troops/monsters. Order-of-Dawn buildings give huge scores/buffs: Dawn Temple (defeat=3000; heals 30% of server wounded + whole-server ATK/DEF/HP +150% for 10min; attacking it blocks your teleport 5min), Dawn Sanctuary (1500; +100% 5min), Solar Eclipse Stronghold x4 (1000; March Size +150% 10min), Lunar Eclipse Stronghold x4 (1000; Rally Capacity +50% 10min). Empire City: occupy an enemy's for 3-20/sec (40M-120M troop power); conquered after 30min total occupation, giving +15% to your total score at battle end. Empire Turret (500/continuous min), Empire Rally Hall x10 (+15% server rally capacity; 500/min), Empire Battlefield Hospital x10 (+60% server healing; 500/min), Blessing Tower x10 (+20% server ATK/DEF; 16/continuous min), Temple of Civilization center (10-40/sec by power). Enemy-troop kills score per 1,000,000 killed, scaling by troop level (Lv1=4 up to Lv15=280). Monster/boss kills give score + limited buffs (Lv1 Shadow of Dawn=10 up to Typhon boss=420). Rewards are modest vs other battlefields; both server-rank and personal-rank rewards.

title: What is KE (Kill Event)?
summary: KE = 'Kill Enemies', the final stage of the Monarch Competition event. Score by defeating enemy troops; reward is a 3-level personal quota scaled to your level so even low players can complete it.
content: KE (Kill Event) = the 'Kill Enemies' stage, the final stage of the 'Monarch Competition' event (5 stages solo; the cross-server 'Server Monarch Competition' has 6 stages with stage 6 = Kill Enemies). It is distinct from 'Server War' (SvS): Server War scores purely by how many enemy soldiers you defeat on enemy servers, whereas KE/Kill-Enemies also has other scoring paths. Reward = a 3-level quota scaled to the player's level (low-level players can still achieve it) and the reward contents scale with level. Example for Keep 27: quota-1 = Troop March Speedup x3 + 10-Stamina x3 + 30min Heal Speedup x2; quota-2 = Adv City Teleporter x2 + Research Stone x5 + 30min Training Speedup x2; quota-3 = 1,770 Gems + Refining Stone x25 + 100 Chips.

title: How to Find Enemies Easily in Server War
summary: Reliable SvS targeting method: use the enemy-server ranking + the 'Golden Sword' marker (enemy currently on YOUR server) + Locate Coordinates, then teleport in. Includes decoy/timing cautions to avoid wasting Arrest Warrants.
content: In SvS most enemies are shielded, burned by purge, or intercepted, so finding attackable targets is hard. BEST method: Event Center > Server War > Ranking of Enemy Server > Alliance Power Ranking > pick an alliance > View Members > find members with the 'Golden Sword' symbol (means they are currently on YOUR server) > Detail > Locate Coordinates (consumes an Arrest Warrant, otherwise costs gems) > teleport in and attack. Fighting an enemy who is on your own server = relative advantage (you can shield, and they get fewer reinforcements). Golden-Sword players usually leave your server quickly, so search immediately; players who stay a very long time are likely decoys - note and ignore them. Before spending an Arrest Warrant, check around your own temple (you may find enemies for free) and watch world chat for decoy intel. TIMING after locating: do NOT teleport if the enemy has an orange shield (teleporting onto a server auto-shields for the first 5 minutes); if no dotted march line extends from their city, wait and teleport only when they are out attacking a monster/city (so they can't escape) but land just before their troops return home (else they call friends); if there are no cities near them they will likely re-teleport away.

title: Relic - How to Gain More or Explore Faster (Verification Report)
summary: Relic-tile exploration mechanics (a gathering activity, not warfare scoring): only relic LEVEL and Alliance research raise yield/speed. Generals, skills, equipment, items, power, gathering-speed and gathering-amount all have NO effect.
content: There are only two ways to raise relic yield-per-hour: (1) explore a HIGHER-LEVEL relic, (2) Alliance research. Speed by level: Mysterious Relics 390/5s per player > Lv5 300 > Lv4 270 - so prefer Mysterious Relics only (Keep 20+ required to enter; bring one out with a 'Magic Treasure Map', composed Common>Uncommon>Rare>Legendary>Epic>Magic). Research: Alliance > Mysterious Relic Exploration (+20% max) and Adv Mysterious Relic Exploration (+20% max); the boost applies to resources, fragments, hammers and items (increases total item count). Verified to have NO effect on relic yield: general choice/level, skills, troop type/level, equipment, monarch gear, items, power, troop-load, resource-gathering-speed, and resource-gathering-amount-boost. (A troop-load skill inflating the pre-march gather time is a glitch - no real gain; do not send Attila/Amir Timur for relics.) Adding more players to a relic only clears it faster; it does not raise per-player speed. Note: this 'relic' is map relic-tile gathering, not an SvS/event relic currency.

title: Rally Spot
summary: Building that sets your maximum March Size (troops per single march). Built at Keep 1, scales with Keep level. Lv35 unlocks a Duty Officer slot that adds march-size capacity. Distinct from War Hall (rally capacity).
content: The Rally Spot raises your maximum March Size (the number of troops you can send in one march). Buildable from Keep 1 and scales with Keep level. At Rally Spot Lv35 a Duty Officer slot unlocks that grants +March Size Capacity - ideal officers are Toyotomi Hideyoshi, Niwa Nagahide or Zhang Liang (enable extra skills/specialties); any golden historic general works otherwise. March size is also raised by march-size-increase items, research, generals, VIP, etc., and is separately capped by Keep level. IMPORTANT distinction: Rally Spot = your march SIZE; the War Hall = rally CAPACITY (number of participants your own rally accepts) - they are different buildings.

title: War Hall
summary: Building that raises RALLY CAPACITY (max participants your own rally accepts). Buildable at Keep 9; each level requires the matching Embassy level. Also hosts the Alliance War screen that shows incoming/outgoing rallies and scouts.
content: The War Hall increases rally capacity = the maximum number of participants you can accept when YOU start a rally. Built at Keep 9; each War Hall level requires the Embassy at the same level. Its 'Alliance War' menu (same as Alliance button > Alliance War) shows every rally or scout made by allies or enemies - i.e. the incoming-rally/scout monitor. NOTE (corrects an earlier assumption): War Hall DOES exist in Evony: The King's Return and is distinct from the Rally Spot (which sets your march size). All War Hall menus stay available during upgrades.

title: Chest Contents (Battlefield, SVS)
summary: Reward inventory for Battlefield (Gaugamela/Constantinople/Chalons) and Server War chests - the game's richest gem/gold source. Full SvS personal-score goals = 236,250 gems; BoC gives thousands of gems even on a loss.
content: Server War and Battlefield chests are the most lucrative gem/gold source in the game. Achieving all SvS personal-score goals yields 236,250 Gems; the Battle of Constantinople gives thousands of gems even if you lose. Battlefield chests come in Resource / Speedup / Gold / Gem variants per battle (Battle of Gaugamela, Constantinople, and Chalons) plus the 'Ultimate War Treasure'. Each chest gives at least one item from a listed pool - e.g. Gaugamela Gold chest: 1M/500K/200Kx2/100Kx3 Gold; Gaugamela Speedup chest: 12h/8h/3hx2/60minx3. SvS packages: 'Server War Monarch Score Package' and 'Server War Alliance Score Package'. (SvS chest contents were updated 2023/10/10.)

title: Battlefield Shop Item List
summary: The Battlefield shop spends Badges (or Dragon Crystals): Epic Historic General, scrolls, gems, gold, resources, Monarch/General EXP, and Lv5/Lv6 materials.
content: Battlefield shop currency = Badges (or Dragon Crystals, 'dc'). Sample prices: Epic Historic General x1 = 2,040 Badges / 6,000dc (can roll either tavern or non-tavern generals); King's Scroll Chest = 360 Badges, Dragon Scroll Chest = 1,160 Badges; Gems 500/1,000/2,000/5,000 = 10/20/40/110 Badges; Gold 200K/500K/1M = 20/40/90 Badges (1M x10 = 850); Resources 2M/5M/20M/50M = 30/60/260/640 Badges; Monarch EXP and General EXP bundles; Lv5 (Orange) and Lv6 (Gold) materials; a 'Special Item' section; plus an added 'Huns Invasion' battlefield section. Spend Badges earned from battlefields on the Epic Historic General and scrolls first for best value.

title: Arctic Barbarians Invasion Guide
summary: Pseudo-PvP-vs-NPC event: defeat and OCCUPY Barbarian Castles (Lv1-6) for score. Solo or rally; only 10 self-defeats/day; losses = wounded (not dead) and wound-adjusted low; net resource-positive; big gem source (500M personal = 189,000 gems).
content: A pseudo-PvP event against NPC 'Arctic Barbarian's Castle' that appears randomly on the map. Combat can be solo OR rally, but you can only defeat castles yourself 10 times per day (losses don't reduce the count) - so castle selection matters. Losses become WOUNDED troops (main city -> hospital, siege -> trap factory), NOT dead; no deserters/souls, so no Horns of Recalling or Soul Crystals needed. The event is wound-adjusted (far fewer wounded than normal PvP - a barbarian side losing ~1M may only wound tens of thousands of yours) and is net resource-POSITIVE (Lv4 ~5-8M per resource type per kill, Lv5 ~8-12M), effectively a resource-earning event; Lv6 is only profitable at Keep 45+. Score mainly comes from OCCUPYING a defeated castle for a period (defeating alone gives little). Three reward tracks: personal-score-achievement (non-competitive - guaranteed if you grind), personal-score rank, and alliance rank. Reaching 500M personal score = 189,000 Gems. Tips: get alliance members to help, have friends take the weaker castles, 'Don't rally' (solo is more efficient here), and if you only need 500M you can just defeat Lv5 (836M+) castles without occupying. Event usually lasts ~11 days.

title: Subordinate City Complete Guide
summary: Full subcity guide: unlock at Keep 11, up to 9. Real value = Buff / Debuff / General-EXP (troops, gold, materials are minor). Rarity upgraded with gems; mayor = a debuff general; combat 'auto fight'/'accept reinforcements' settings decide ghost vs defend.
content: Unlocked at Keep 11 (first in-city subcity + you can occupy outer NPC subcities; up to 9 total, raised via Rank buffs e.g. Archduke +6). The REAL benefits are Buff (culture), Debuff (mayor) and General-EXP; troop/gold/material production are minor extras (subcity troops are low-tier and, when killed, DIE rather than wound unless survival research is up). COMBAT SETTINGS per subcity: 'Automatic fight for other Cities' (this subcity auto-joins defense of your main/other subcities) and 'Accept Reinforcements' (others auto-join to defend this subcity) - turn ON only if confident of winning the defense; if you are losing or GHOSTING, turn them OFF (else subcity troops die for nothing). Culture buffs apply regardless of these settings. Recommended policy = 'war-oriented' (fast troop training so the mayor survives). To bring a subcity INTO an attack, select it on the pre-march troop screen (linkage settings are irrelevant for that). You CANNOT bring a subcity in when: no mayor, subcity troops = 0, a rally, a temple attack, a battlefield-building attack, or an NPC-subcity attack. RARITY (Common/Uncommon/Excellent/Legendary/Epic): higher = stronger buffs (most important), faster training, higher troop cap, faster gold. Upgrade only the in-city subcity (map subcities can't upgrade): Uncommon=subcity Keep10+5,000 gems, Excellent=Keep15+50,000, Legendary=Keep20+500,000, Epic=Keep25+2,500,000 gems. HOW TO GET: (A) beat an NPC subcity once - solo only, no rally, can't bring an owned subcity; NPC power ranges White ~21.6K to Gold ~9.1M and your kills there are DEATHS; (B) rob a player's subcity - drop wall HP to 0 and attack 10 times, solo only; scouting won't reveal if their main city reinforces, so probe with a small weak force first; (C) 'Historic City Search' event - bet Subordinate City Clues, and on winning, USING the key announces you server-wide and you CANNOT bubble for 10 minutes after occupying (use a name-change trick to dodge interception). MAYOR = a historic general WITH a debuff (skill/equipment/specialty); the mayor's buffs do NOT help main-city troops (only subcity troops), but the mayor's DEBUFF applies whenever the subcity joins a battle and all mayors' debuffs STACK. At Keep 33+ the mayor's Achaemenidae debuff makes even one subcity matter, so keep the 'Death into Survival Rate in this Subordinate City' buff active (mayor Leadership & Politics >=900) so subcity troops survive to keep debuffing. Add subcity-category skills in priority Rebirth > Training Speed > Training Capacity. Abandoning a subcity reverts it to a Lv1 NPC subcity; re-occupying restores its pre-abandon level. Subcities cannot be moved (fixed map locations).

title: Important Usage of Subordinate City
summary: The three real subcity benefits (Buff / Debuff / General-EXP) explained: culture buff types for battle vs development, same-culture stacking (9 Japan Epic = +90% attack), mayor=debuff doctrine, and the culture-vs-culture-feature nuance.
content: The three real benefits of subcities are Buff, Debuff, and General-EXP. BUFFS stack: each owned subcity adds its buff and same-culture subcities stack (9 Japanese Epic = +90% marching attack; 9 Korean Epic = +135% gathering); effectiveness scales with rarity. Culture for BATTLE: Japan = Main-City Attacking (marching) Troop Attack + research-factory material speed; Russia = In-City (defending) Troop Attack + Trap Attack; Arabia = Hospital Capacity + Healing Speed. Culture for DEVELOPMENT: Korea (gathering + warehouse), Europe (construction + monster-kill general-EXP), China (main-city RSS production + training), America (subcity gold + research). Pick a single culture matching your current goal and re-roll culture as you progress (development early, battle later). The in-city subcity's 'subcity buff' is NOT the same as the 'Culture Feature' bonus - choose culture for the subcity-buff (e.g. America for research speed, not China). DEBUFF: a mayor with a debuff (attack/HP down via skill/equipment/specialty) projects into any battle the subcity joins; all mayors' debuffs STACK (top players reach -600% including equipment and specialty). Doctrine: 'the general who should be a subcity mayor = a general with a debuff skill.' Also give the mayor debuff Red equipment (some Ares / Achaemenidae) or a debuff dragon (Norway Ridge). The mayor's own attack/defense buffs do NOT reach the main city, so ignore them. GENERAL-EXP: a mayor brought into battle earns half the main general's EXP (main 10,000 -> mayor 5,000). Gold production and troop training from subcities are minor and not worth investing in.

title: Council of State
summary: Buff facility (Senate). Three buff sources: Military Title grade, Position level, and appointed general's star>=6. Appointed generals are LOCKED (can't march/gather/defend/mayor). Senator titles are gated behind SvS / BoG / BoC scores.
content: Council of State (the Senate) is a facility for obtaining buffs. Built at Keep 1; leveling requires Pasture and Victory Column. Three ways to gain buffs: (1) promote a Military Title grade, (2) level up a Position, (3) raise an appointed general's star to 6+. There are 6 Military Titles x 4 troop-type Positions (Ground/Mounted/Ranged/Siege) = 24 positions. Titles: Governor, Commander, Marshal, Leading Senator, Ruling Senator, Honorary Senator. Appointing a general requires a GOLDEN historic general at Star Level 5+ (purple generals cannot be appointed); higher star = more buff. CRITICAL: appointed generals are LOCKED - while appointed they cannot march for combat/gathering, be a defender, a duty officer, or a subcity mayor. Governor/Commander/Marshal positions unlock by Council level (Governor-Ground L1 up to Marshal-Siege L30). Senator titles are gated behind WARFARE scores: Leading Senator = win the Server War and earn Monarch Scores (540M/700M/1B/2B, several times); Ruling Senator = win the Battle of Gaugamela personal scores; Honorary Senator = win the Battle of Constantinople personal scores. Buffs = troop Attack/Defense/HP plus enemy debuffs (a fully-leveled Governor totals roughly +13% attack and up to -9% enemy defense per troop type). Costs: Position level-up = Merit Jade + Glory Golden Twig (from boss-hunting, capped 15 Merit Jade / 6 Golden Twig per day, plus daily activity and packs); Military Title promotion = Glory Golden Twig; Position promotion = Historic Medal / Blood of Ares / Radiant Medal (Radiant Medal only from King's Party = heavy spending).

title: List of Subordinate City Buff
summary: Exact per-culture subcity buff values by rarity (Common/Uncommon/Excellent/Legendary/Epic). Buffs stack across same-culture cities (8 Russia Epic = +80% in-city attack). Two common buffs (training / death-into-survival) require mayor conditions.
content: Per-culture subcity buffs, values Common/Uncommon/Excellent/Legendary/Epic. Europe: Main City Construction Speed +3/6/9/12/15%; General EXP from Monster Kill +2/4/6/8/10%. China: Main City Resource Production Speed +4/8/12/16/20%; Training Speed +2/4/6/8/10%. Japan: Main City Attacking (marching) Troop Attack +2/4/6/8/10%; Research Factory Material Production Speed +1/2/3/4/5%. Korea: Main City Warehouse Capacity +6/12/18/24/30%; Extra Resources from Gathering +3/6/9/12/15%. America: Current Subcity Gold Production Speed +10/20/30/40/50%; Research Speed +3/6/9/12/15%. Russia: In-City (defending) Troop Attack +2/4/6/8/10%; Trap Attack +3/6/9/12/15%. Arabia: Hospital Capacity +3/6/9/12/15%; Healing Speed +2/4/6/8/10%. Common (any culture): Training Speed in this subcity +2/5/10/15/20% (requires mayor = a historic general); Death-into-Survival Rate in this subcity +2/4/6/8/10% (requires mayor Leadership & Politics >=900). Buffs STACK across same-culture cities: 8 Russia Epic = +80% in-city troop attack. For battle, Japan (marching attack = offense) vs Russia (in-city attack = defense) - pick per role.

title: Ghosting Complete Guide (Essential PvP Techniques)
summary: Ghosting = send all troops out on 60-minute rallies so an attacked empty keep loses ~no troops (only wall/traps take damage). Core SvS/battlefield survival when you cannot bubble. Rally to a BOSS to keep the shield up; recall instantly to defend/teleport.
content: Ghosting is a defensive technique: zero out the troops in your keep (and Rally Spot) so an attack kills almost no troops (walls and traps still take damage). Use it when you cannot bubble: immediately after entering the Battle of Constantinople/Gaugamela, or when staying a long time on an enemy server in SvS (you cannot put a shield on an enemy server). METHOD: rally surrounding cities/buildings/boss monsters for 60 minutes to drop keep troops to 0; turn OFF subcity 'automatic fight for other cities' (else subcity troops die); spend resources or bank them in the Alliance Warehouse, keeping on-hand below the warehouse-protected amount. In SvS you MUST resign the defense general before ghosting or he DIES when attacked (true since 10/3/2021); in BoG/BoC the defense general does NOT die, so you may leave him on. RALLY (not a plain march) because cancelling a rally returns troops to the keep INSTANTLY so you can defend/attack/teleport; you cannot teleport while marching or during a rally (cancel all rallies first). TARGET CHOICE: rally a BOSS MONSTER (costs stamina) rather than a city - rallying a city/temple/throne DEACTIVATES your shield and alerts the enemy to your exact troop count/type, while rallying a boss keeps the shield UP and hides your strength. If you must use a city (e.g. BoC has no bosses), pick a NON-allied city (the target's allies would otherwise see your formation). IF ATTACKED while ghosting: walls still lose HP and at 0 HP (or ~10 hits; 3 hits in BoG/BoC) you are 'sent flying' to a random spot and ALL rallied troops return to the keep - decide instantly to re-ghost or teleport (some enemies zero your HP, Arrest-Warrant-search you, and chase the returned troops). If the boss/city you rallied disappears, the ghost stays intact. PREP: bookmark ghost targets (bosses and non-allied cities) in advance; you cannot ghost to targets on your own server while on an enemy server. A scout of a ghosted city reports 0 troops. COUNTERATTACK: if you think you can win, cancel the ghost the moment you are attacked and win on defense (advanced: switch subcity auto-fight ON to add mayor debuffs). BAIT ('dare to leave some'): leave a few troops or one troop type to look weak, then recall the rest the instant the enemy commits.
